summary: Add a local-only single-admin password gate with session cookie and protected admin routes.
acceptance_criteria: A /login page exists for the local single-admin workflow and does not create users, accounts, signup, OAuth, email login, or multi-tenant ownership.
A server-side auth helper validates a local admin password from environment configuration and issues/verifies a session cookie without exposing secrets to client bundles or logs.
Middleware protects /dashboard, /radar-tasks, and /opportunities and redirects unauthenticated requests to /login while allowing login/auth API routes and static assets.
Logout is available and clears the local session cookie.
README and .env.example document the local-only admin variables and keep production auth/deployment decisions human-gated.
Focused automated tests cover password validation, session signing/verification or equivalent cookie behavior, middleware route classification, and no-secret defaults.
npm test and npm run build pass.
notes: Derived from docs/11_development_plan.md M2 and the 2026-06-29 human-approved Phase 0B backfill scope.
Security review is the primary reviewer because this task introduces session cookies and route protection.
Keep this local-only and reversible. Do not introduce external auth providers or new dependencies.
non_goals: No production-grade multi-user auth, OAuth, email login, signup, account management, team permissions, or ownership checks.
No public deployment, DNS, CI/CD, paid provider, real account, billing, telemetry, cron, or email work.
No Prisma schema migration, seed change, provider change, scan orchestration change, scoring change, or package dependency change.
No Radar Task CRUD UI beyond links needed for protected navigation.
